channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US carries out 25 executions this year as death penalty trends in nation held steady
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — The number of executions in the U.S. remained near historic lows in 2024 and was mostly carried out in a small group of states, including Alabama, which became the first state to use nitrogen gas as an execution method, according to an annual report on capital punishment.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Johnson spending deal throws Speakership into question as floor vote approaches
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson’s handling of an end-of-year spending deal is throwing his grasp on the gavel into uncertain territory ahead of a critical Jan. 3 Speaker vote, as some GOP lawmakers question their support for the Louisiana Republican. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers caught off guard by Trump debt ceiling demand
item.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the aisle appeared caught off guard on Wednesday as President-elect Trump demanded that a debt ceiling hike be paired with a stopgap government funding bill, rejecting a sweeping bipartisan plan to avert a government shutdown this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: New Year's Eve ball drop numbers unveiled in NYC
item.description: Even though New Year’s Eve is nearly two weeks away, there was an official celebration in the location best known worldwide for ringing in the new year.
item.dc:creator: James Ford, KRON4

item.title: Immigration drives US population growth to highest rate in 23 years as residents pass 340 million
item.description: Immigration in 2024 drove U.S. population growth to its fastest rate in 23 years as the nation surpassed 340 million residents.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court removes prosecutor Fani Willis from Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others but did not dismiss the indictment, leaving the future of the prosecution uncertain.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harmful gas billowing from Texas and New Mexico comes mostly from smaller leaks, researchers say
item.description: The blob on the satellite image is a rainbow of colors. An analyst digitally sharpens it and there, highlighted in red, is the source: a concrete oil pad spewing methane.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Fox 5

item.title: Former Uvalde schools police chief loses bid to toss criminal charges related to 2022 shooting
item.description: A Texas judge has refused to throw out criminal charges accusing the former Uvalde schools police chief of endangering children in the slow law enforcement response to the 2022 Robb Elementary shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How New York prosecutors used a terrorism law in the charges against Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors are using a 9/11-era anti-terrorism law in their case against the man charged with gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Commerce Department's efforts to curb China's and Russia's access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons, according to a report published Wednesday by the Senate's Perman
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to appear at contempt hearing in January over failure to give up assets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered Rudy Giuliani to appear in court in early January to face claims that he should be held in contempt for failing to turn over assets including a New York apartment lease and a signed Joe DiMaggio shirt to two Georgia election poll workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: The video shows an employee cutting up or hammering meat on the sidewalk or drive-thru pavement area right outside Tryyaki.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Denver couple sets out fake packages to deter porch pirates
item.description: One Denver couple is trying to combat the rise in package theft in their own way -- by leaving fake packages out for thieves to take.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man found guilty of murdering mother
item.description: Dillon Alexander Waller, 29, was found guilty by a Las Cruces jury and will be facing life in prison with the possibility of parole after 30 years, the Third Judicial DA said.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Flores, NewsNation

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor expected in court on criminal charges, AP sources say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some elite US universities favor wealthy students in admissions decisions, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Children of the wealthy and connected get special admissions consideration at some elite U.S. universities, according to new filings in a class-action lawsuit originally brought against 17 schools.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security bill clears first hurdle in Senate
item.description: The Senate voted 73-27 to advance the measure, which could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans if passed before Congress leaves this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico tightens bonds with immigrant community in US
item.description: The government of Mexico is strengthening bonds with its citizens in the United States, pledging to assist them at a time immigrant communities could be coming under increased scrutiny here.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Idaho murder victim's mom breaks silence two years after daughter's killing
item.description: University of Idaho murder victim Madison Mogen's mother spoke publicly for the first time since the quadruple homicide that was allegedly carried out by Bryan Kohberger.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments sells for over $5M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments sold for more than $5 million at an auction on Wednesday. Sotheby’s said the 155-pound (52-kilogram) marble slab was acquired by an anonymous buyer who plans to donate it to an Israeli institution.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, already stuck in space, are hit with new delay
item.description: Astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, the crew of the first manned Boeing Starliner launch in early June, were initially meant to spend only about eight days in space.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenge to TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court announced it will take up whether a law that could ban TikTok nationwide violates the First Amendment, setting the stage for a fast-paced, high-stakes battle over free speech.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists find 'forever chemicals' lurking in certain smartwatch wristbands
item.description: Prior research has shown that more than a fifth of Americans wear a smartwatch or a fitness tracker — and they do so for extended periods of time.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts face more time in space with return delayed until at least late March
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA's two stuck astronauts just got their space mission extended again. That means they won't be back until spring, 10 months after rocketing into orbit on Boeing's Starliner capsule.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 1 dead, 9 injured in Maryland shooting and fiery car crash: police
item.description: One person has died and nine others were injured following a shooting and related fiery car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, Fox 5

item.title: This Colorado restaurant only has 2 items on the menu
item.description: A Michelin-starred chef co-opened a restaurant in Denver with only two items on the menu: a burger and fries.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KRON4

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Paris Hilton asks House to pass bill to prevent child abuse
item.description: The "Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act" has already passed the Senate, and it has the support of celebrity-turned-advocate Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: How to claim a 2nd payment from the Equifax data breach settlement
item.description: Equifax has started alerting people of more money waiting for them from a massive fund the credit reporting agency paid to settle a data breach.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Judge allows testing of tissue from George Floyd as officer convicted of killing him seeks new trial
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A judge has granted permission to lawyers for Derek Chauvin to have samples from George Floyd examined as part of the former Minneapolis police officer's efforts to challenge his conviction on a federal civil rights charge stemming from Floyd's death in 2020. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9
